name: Doppelganger
flavor: A duplicator and trickster who is never quite where the last blow landed, fighting alongside a handful of semi-real reflections of himself.

proficiencies.skills: Choose two from Acrobatics, Deception, Insight, Perception, Sleight of Hand, and Stealth

parryReskin: A Clone lurches into the blow's path at the last possible instant and takes the hit for you — you were never quite where the blade landed, only where you left an afterimage standing. This is pure narration: it does not change the Parry math already resolved (a Grazing Parry or Overcommitted result still deals whatever damage it deals). For a Clone that absorbs a hit entirely, see Mirror Image.
riposte: On a Full Dodge (your Parry d20 roll comes up strictly above your effective Parry DC), you may create one Clone (see your Clone engine) in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you, at no action cost and without spending your bonus action. This follows your normal Clone cap — if you are already at your cap, you must let an existing Clone drop first. A small, thematic reward for turning aside a hit clean, not a counterattack.

engine.name: Clones
engine.description: Semi-real mirror-duplicates of yourself, each occupying its own space, wearing your face and frozen in the pose you struck the moment it was made. A Clone is not a combat pet — it never takes actions of its own — it's a body double for a blade, an eye, or your own attacks to work through. A Clone is a snapshot: it copies every benefit affecting you at the instant it is created (advantage, damage riders, a temporary weapon enchantment, and the like) and keeps them for its whole life even if yours end, but it never gains anything applied to you afterward; its AC equals yours and it has no hit points. Clones do not move on their own once placed — you reshape the picture by spawning new Clones where you want them or by using Swap. Because a Clone is a sliver of your own living body rather than an illusion, it is your Constitution — your raw vitality — that powers your duplicates: your Constitution modifier sets their save DCs and is added to any damage a Clone deals — a Mirror Barrage swing, a Mirror Assault strike, a Decoy Detonation burst — in place of the Dexterity your own strikes use, even as your own blades still strike with a duelist’s Dexterity.
engine.generation: Create one Clone, as a bonus action, in an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of you; it appears already mimicking your exact appearance and current pose. You also gain a Clone at no action cost via Riposte, and via any other feature that says so. You may never have more Clones active at once than your Clone cap; creating a new one beyond your cap requires an existing Clone to drop first.
engine.spend: Clones aren't spent from one fixed pool all at once — specific features use them in specific ways. Swap teleports you into a Clone's space without destroying it. Mirror Barrage has every Clone in reach make one half-damage attack, without destroying them. Mirror Image destroys a Clone within 5 feet to fully absorb a hit that would otherwise land on you. Decoy Detonation destroys a Clone to burst it for damage and knockback. Mirror Assault (5th level) consumes a Clone to deliver an extra attack from its space. A Clone also drops on its own, destroying no other resource, the moment it ends up more than 30 feet from you, the instant you become incapacitated (all your Clones drop at once), or the first time any attack roll succeeds against it — it shares your AC but has no hit points of its own, so one confirmed hit and it's gone.
engine.cap: proficiency bonus (2 at levels 1-4, 3 at level 5; Hall of Mirrors adds 1 more starting at 5th level)

features.name: Clones
features.description: You gain the Clones engine. As a bonus action, you create one Clone — a semi-real mirror-duplicate of yourself, mimicking your appearance and pose — in an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet (45 at 5th level). At the instant it appears, a Clone copies your exact state: every benefit currently affecting you (advantage, damage riders, a temporary weapon enchantment, and so on) carries onto the Clone and stays for as long as it exists, even after your own version ends; it does not gain anything applied to you later. You can have up to your Clone cap (your proficiency bonus) active at once; creating one beyond the cap drops an existing Clone. A Clone shares your AC but has no hit points — a single hit destroys it — does not move on its own once placed, and drops if it ends more than 30 feet from you or you become incapacitated. Your other features reposition and spend Clones; see your Clone engine for the full detail.

features.name: Mirror Barrage
features.description: You act, and every reflection acts with you. As a bonus action, each of your Clones that has a creature within its reach makes one weapon attack from its own space against a target it can reach, using your attack roll and whatever weapon and benefits it copied when it was created. On a hit, a Clone deals half its damage, rounded down — and because a Clone strikes with your vitality rather than your aim, it adds your Constitution modifier where your own attack would add Dexterity: roll the weapon's damage die, add your Constitution modifier and any riders the Clone copied, then halve the total. Several Clones positioned around the same target all strike it; Clones spread across different enemies each strike their own. The Clones are not destroyed by attacking — they simply swing. You can use Mirror Barrage once per turn.

features.name: Mirror Image
features.description: Your Clones double as decoys that can take a hit meant for you. Whenever a creature you can see hits you with an attack, then — before you decide whether to Parry — roll a d6 to see if a decoy takes the blow instead. The redirect chance rises with how many Clones you have out: one Clone redirects on a 1 (1-in-6), two Clones on a 1-2 (2-in-6), three Clones on a 1-3 (3-in-6, the maximum). If it redirects: the attack strikes a Clone within 5 feet of you, that Clone is destroyed, you take no damage, and your reaction stays free (this doesn't use it). If it doesn't redirect: the hit lands as normal and you may still spend your reaction to Parry it. Mirror Image sits in front of your Parry as a bonus chance to avoid a hit entirely — a supplement, never a replacement.

features.name: Swap
features.description: As a bonus action, you may teleport-swap places with one of your Clones within 30 feet of you: you instantly appear in the Clone's space and it instantly appears in yours, each facing however you choose. Neither of you provokes opportunity attacks from this movement, and the Clone itself is not destroyed — it simply continues existing wherever it ends up, still bound by its normal leash and still able to be hit, redirected onto, or spent like any other Clone. You can use Swap once per turn.

features.name: Decoy Detonation
features.description: As an action, you cause one of your Clones to burst in a flash of shattering reflections, destroying it. Because you choose which Clone, you decide where the blast lands — spawn a Clone into a cluster of enemies, or Swap one there, then detonate it. Each creature within 5 feet of that Clone must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier). On a failed save a creature takes [[1d6]] plus your Constitution modifier psychic damage and is shoved 5 feet straight away from the Clone's space; on a success it takes half as much damage and is not moved. From 3rd level, Splintered Reflexes also lets you trigger Decoy Detonation as a reaction on another creature's turn. You can trigger Decoy Detonation at most once per turn.

features.name: Flanking Reflection
features.description: When you make a melee weapon attack against a creature that has one of your Clones within 5 feet of it, you have advantage on the attack roll — the creature can't watch both you and your reflection at once. This costs nothing and doesn't destroy the Clone, but requires one to already be positioned correctly; it applies at most once per attack and doesn't stack with other sources of advantage on the same roll.

features.name: Splintered Reflexes
features.description: Your reflections let you answer more than one threat at once. You gain one additional reaction each round, for two total, both refreshing at the start of your turn. This extra reaction can be used only to make an opportunity attack or to activate a Clone feature that costs a reaction — most usefully, it lets you trigger Decoy Detonation on another creature's turn. It can never be used to Parry a second time: you still redirect only one hit per round onto a Clone, so the Acrobat remains the surer dodger. Each reaction is spent once; you choose which threat each one answers.

features.name: Mirror Assault
features.description: This is your 5th-level damage step — the Doppelganger's version of Extra Attack, but earned rather than free. Once per turn, when you take the Attack action, if one of your Clones is within 5 feet of your target, you can make one extra weapon attack that strikes from that Clone's space, using your normal attack roll; its damage is the weapon's die plus your Constitution modifier (a Clone's blow draws on your vitality, not your Dexterity). That Clone is then destroyed — it spends itself delivering the blow. Unlike a plain Extra Attack this one is conditional: you need a Clone already positioned next to the target, and it costs you that Clone.

features.name: Hall of Mirrors
features.description: Your reflections run deeper and reach farther: your Clone cap increases by 1 (in addition to whatever your proficiency bonus already grants it), and the range on Clone creation, Swap, and a Clone's leash all increase from 30 to 45 feet.

keyStats.label: Save DC
keyStats.note: Save DC for effects your features force (e.g. Decoy Detonation).

keyStats.label: Clones Cap
keyStats.note: Most active Clone tokens you can have.

keyStats.label: Reactions
keyStats.note: Second reaction: opportunity attack or clone reaction only, never a 2nd Parry.
